title: Automated tests

publication.changeSummary: Language-specific source-linked draft lesson for Automated tests; independent technical, source, pedagogy, accessibility, and runnable-example review remains required. Beginner-quality contract 1.0.0 applied deterministically from existing source-linked draft facts; publication stage and reviewer state are unchanged.

sources.claim: Python automated tests isolate fixtures, call observable contracts, and assert normal, boundary, and failure behavior; mocks belong at genuine external or nondeterministic boundaries.

canonicalExplanation.summary: Python automated tests isolate fixtures, call observable contracts, and assert normal, boundary, and failure behavior; mocks belong at genuine external or nondeterministic boundaries.

sections.heading: Start here
sections.body: Start with this case: A table of subtests covers empty, normal, Unicode, and malformed cases, while assertRaises checks the exact domain failure. Your goal is to create a deterministic unit and integration test suite covering normal, boundary and failure behavior. Work through the example one step at a time, then use the boundary to check your answer.

sections.heading: Before you begin
sections.body: Review these declared prerequisites first: Project metadata and command entry; Exceptions, files and resources; Classes and object protocols; Raising and custom exceptions; Packages and public APIs. If one is unfamiliar, revisit it before continuing.

sections.heading: Language-specific operational model
sections.body: Python automated tests isolate fixtures, call observable contracts, and assert normal, boundary, and failure behavior; mocks belong at genuine external or nondeterministic boundaries. This model is applied through the declared Python: Beginner to Intermediate behavior rather than a language-neutral slogan.

sections.heading: Boundary and failure model
sections.body: Test order should not carry state, patch targets must match lookup location, broad mocks can reproduce implementation rather than behavior, and coverage measures execution rather than assertion quality. The learner must reject this documented misconception: More mocks always make a unit test better isolated and high coverage proves all contracts are tested.

includes: Use isolated fixtures, assertions, subtests and mocks only at genuine external boundaries.
Create a deterministic unit and integration test suite covering normal, boundary and failure behavior.
excludes: Test order should not carry state, patch targets must match lookup location, broad mocks can reproduce implementation rather than behavior, and coverage measures execution rather than assertion quality.

outcomes: Create a deterministic unit and integration test suite covering normal, boundary and failure behavior.

examples.title: Automated tests: direct language case
examples.situation: A table of subtests covers empty, normal, Unicode, and malformed cases, while assertRaises checks the exact domain failure.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Observe the concrete Python: Beginner to Intermediate state or tool result: A table of subtests covers empty, normal, Unicode, and malformed cases, while assertRaises checks the exact domain failure.
Step 2 — Use the core rule above to predict one observable result.

Step 3 — Verify the result with this correction: Assert public behavior with deterministic fixtures, isolate true external boundaries, and derive cases from equivalence classes, boundaries, and failures.
examples.result: The example meets the atomic outcome because Assert public behavior with deterministic fixtures, isolate true external boundaries, and derive cases from equivalence classes, boundaries, and failures.

examples.title: Automated tests: boundary and transfer case
examples.situation: A clock collaborator is injected and controlled, but the test asserts the returned expiration policy rather than private call sequence.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Name what changed from the direct case: A clock collaborator is injected and controlled, but the test asserts the returned expiration policy rather than private call sequence.

Step 2 — Keep the language boundary explicit: Test order should not carry state, patch targets must match lookup location, broad mocks can reproduce implementation rather than behavior, and coverage measures execution rather than assertion quality.
Step 3 — Reject the tempting but incorrect rule: More mocks always make a unit test better isolated and high coverage proves all contracts are tested.
examples.result: This case demonstrates the declared outcome: Create a deterministic unit and integration test suite covering normal, boundary and failure behavior.

misconceptions.mistakenBelief: More mocks always make a unit test better isolated and high coverage proves all contracts are tested.
misconceptions.correction: Assert public behavior with deterministic fixtures, isolate true external boundaries, and derive cases from equivalence classes, boundaries, and failures.
misconceptions.diagnosticPrompt: Predict both examples and identify the first observable contradiction in this belief: More mocks always make a unit test better isolated and high coverage proves all contracts are tested.

analogy.example: an inspected delivery package whose interface notes, repeatable checks, safe incident log, and clean installation prove bounded quality claims can introduce Automated tests, after the canonical Python: Beginner to Intermediate rule is stated first.
usefulFor: typing, tests, logging, debugging, and project completion
limitations: annotations do not enforce runtime types, mocks can mislead, logs can leak, and local editable installs can hide packaging defects

recap.summary: You should now be able to create a deterministic unit and integration test suite covering normal, boundary and failure behavior. Keep this boundary in view: Test order should not carry state, patch targets must match lookup location, broad mocks can reproduce implementation rather than behavior, and coverage measures execution rather than assertion quality.
